title: Review hub + narrative review
analysis:
Task 46 builds the review hub at /review and narrative review page at /review/narratives with full CRUD + approve/reject/edit functionality via HTMX.

Existing patterns to follow:
- FastAPI with APIRouter, Jinja2Templates, HTMX 2.0.4
- `_get_db(request)` helper returns CatalogDB from app.state.db_path, closed in finally
- `_is_htmx(request)` checks hx-request header for dual HTML/JSON response
- Routes registered via __init__.py exports → app.py include_router()
- Templates extend base.html with {% block content %}, dark theme with Tailwind
- Partials in templates/partials/ for HTMX swap targets
- File-backed tmp_path DB in test fixtures (web app creates its own connection)

DB layer: CatalogDB already has insert_narrative, get_narrative, update_narrative_status, list_narratives. Missing: update_narrative(**kwargs) for field editing (title, description, duration). Need to add this method following the same pattern as update_gate/update_activity_cluster.

Gate integration: gates.py already has POST /api/gates/{stage}/approve. The review hub needs to show waiting gates and link to review pages. The narrative review page needs an 'Approve Gate' button that calls the existing gate approve endpoint when all narratives are decided.

The base.html nav already has links to /review, /review/narratives, etc. — they just 404 today.

Backwards compatibility: human_review_fn in CLI remains untouched. Web review is an alternative path writing to the same narratives + pipeline_gates tables.

prerequisites.description: Add update_narrative(**kwargs) method to CatalogDB for general field updates (title, description, proposed_duration_seconds, etc.) following the same pattern as update_gate and update_activity_cluster

steps.description: Write failing tests for CatalogDB.update_narrative: updates title, description, proposed_duration_seconds; leaves unmentioned fields unchanged; no-ops on empty kwargs
Implement update_narrative(**kwargs) in CatalogDB following update_gate pattern: build SET clause dynamically, execute UPDATE
Write failing test: review_router is importable from autopilot.web.routes and app includes it; GET /review returns 200
Create autopilot/web/routes/review.py with router = APIRouter(), stub /review route. Update __init__.py to export review_router. Update app.py to include_router(review_router)
Write failing tests for GET /api/narratives: returns JSON list; filters by ?status=proposed; returns empty list when no narratives
Implement GET /api/narratives endpoint returning db.list_narratives() with optional status query param
Write failing tests for GET /api/narratives/{id}: returns narrative dict with parsed cluster IDs; returns 404 for nonexistent id
Implement GET /api/narratives/{id} endpoint with db.get_narrative(), parse activity_cluster_ids_json, return 404 if missing
Write failing tests for POST /api/narratives/{id}/approve: sets status=approved, returns updated narrative JSON; 404 for missing id
Implement POST /api/narratives/{id}/approve: call db.update_narrative_status(id, 'approved'), return updated narrative or HTML partial if HTMX
Write failing tests for POST /api/narratives/{id}/reject: sets status=rejected, returns updated narrative JSON; 404 for missing id
Implement POST /api/narratives/{id}/reject: call db.update_narrative_status(id, 'rejected'), return updated narrative or HTML partial if HTMX
Write failing tests for PUT /api/narratives/{id}: updates title/description/duration fields, returns updated narrative; rejects unknown fields; 404 for missing id
Implement PUT /api/narratives/{id} with Pydantic NarrativeUpdate model (optional title, description, proposed_duration_seconds), call db.update_narrative(), return updated narrative
Write failing tests for POST /api/narratives/bulk-approve: accepts JSON list of IDs, approves all, returns count; empty list returns 0
Implement POST /api/narratives/bulk-approve endpoint: iterate IDs, call update_narrative_status for each, return {approved: count}

Write failing test for GET /review hub page: returns 200 HTML; shows 'No pending reviews' when no gates waiting; shows waiting gate info when narrate gate is in 'waiting' status with link to /review/narratives

Implement GET /review route rendering review/hub.html template. Query all gates, filter for status='waiting', for each compute pending review summary (e.g. count of proposed narratives for narrate stage). Create hub.html extending base.html with gate cards or empty state message
Write failing test for GET /review/narratives page: returns 200 HTML; contains proposed narrative titles; contains approve/reject buttons; shows status badges

Implement GET /review/narratives rendering review/narratives.html. Create narratives.html extending base.html with narrative cards grid, each card showing title/description/duration/arc/clusters/status with HTMX approve/reject buttons and checkbox for bulk operations
Write failing test for narrative_card.html partial: POST /api/narratives/{id}/approve with HX-Request header returns HTML containing updated status badge, not JSON

Create partials/narrative_card.html showing narrative fields with status-colored badge, HTMX action buttons (approve/reject/edit), and checkbox. Wire approve/reject endpoints to return this partial when _is_htmx() is true
Write failing test for gate approval integration on narrative review page: when all narratives are approved/rejected, page shows 'Approve Gate' button; button targets POST /api/gates/narrate/approve

Add gate status context to /review/narratives route. In template, conditionally show 'Approve Gate' button when no proposed narratives remain and narrate gate is waiting. Button uses hx-post to existing /api/gates/narrate/approve endpoint
Write failing test for PUT /api/narratives/{id} with HTMX: returns HTML partial with updated fields instead of JSON
Wire PUT endpoint to return narrative_card.html partial when _is_htmx() is true, enabling inline edit save to swap the card in-place

design_decisions.decision: Single route file for both HTML pages and JSON API endpoints
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the pattern in gates.py where /gates (HTML) and /api/gates (JSON) coexist in one file. Keeps review-related routes cohesive.

design_decisions.decision: Add update_narrative(**kwargs) to CatalogDB rather than individual field setters
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the established pattern of update_gate(**kwargs) and update_activity_cluster(**kwargs). Generic field update is more flexible for the PUT endpoint.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse existing POST /api/gates/{stage}/approve from gates.py for gate approval
design_decisions.rationale: The gate approve endpoint already exists and is well-tested. The review page just needs an HTMX button that calls it. No need to duplicate gate logic in review routes.

design_decisions.decision: Separate bulk-approve endpoint instead of overloading single approve
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps API semantics clean. Single approve operates on one narrative, bulk-approve accepts a list. Frontend can use checkboxes + bulk button for efficient multi-select.

design_decisions.decision: Dual response format (JSON vs HTML partial) based on HX-Request header
design_decisions.rationale: Established pattern in gates.py and media.py. HTMX requests get HTML partials for in-place swaps; programmatic/API requests get JSON. Maintains backwards compatibility with both consumers.

design_decisions.decision: Parse activity_cluster_ids_json in API response rather than joining to clusters table
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the API response fast. Cluster details can be fetched separately if needed. The narrative card template only needs cluster IDs for display; full cluster info is not critical for the review UX.

reuse.how: Copy the same _get_db() and _is_htmx() helper functions into review.py. They create CatalogDB from app.state.db_path.
Use existing insert_narrative, get_narrative, update_narrative_status, list_narratives directly. Only need to add update_narrative for field editing.
The Approve Gate button on narrative review page calls the existing gate approve endpoint via HTMX. No new gate logic needed.

reuse.what: base.html template with dark theme, nav, HTMX
reuse.how: New templates extend base.html with {% extends 'base.html' %} and fill {% block content %}. Nav already has /review links.
Define similar _NARRATIVE_STATUS_COLORS dict for proposed=amber, approved=green, rejected=red, scripted=blue, planned=purple.

reuse.what: Test fixture pattern for web routes
reuse.how: Same fixture pattern: file-backed tmp_path DB seeded with test data, create_app(db_path), TestClient(app). Seed narratives and gates for testing.
Create similar _render_narrative_partial helper that renders partials/narrative_card.html for HTMX swap responses.
